positive_description: Extremely subtle deformation. At +3.0, visually indistinguishable from neutral in frontal and 3/4 views. If the alternating pattern continues, this would be a symmetric modulator.
negative_description: Extremely subtle deformation. At -3.0, visually indistinguishable from neutral.
symmetry_notes: Predicted symmetric based on pattern extrapolation (symmetric components at indices 10, 13, 16, 21, 24...). Cannot be confirmed from renders.
artifact_notes: No mesh artifacts at +/-3.0. Safe well beyond tested range.
useful_for: micro-texture in expression blends at very high weights
prose: Psi24 is deep in the expression PCA tail. All seven renders are visually identical to the neutral face across the full -3.0 to +3.0 sweep. If the alternating symmetric/asymmetric architecture from the psi10-20 band continues, psi24 would occupy a symmetric modulator slot — but this extrapolation is increasingly uncertain. At this PCA depth, components likely capture subject-specific residuals from the FLAME training set rather than universal expression modes. The component is functionally inert at any practical expression weight budget.
